2606.13405.title: Neuro-Symbolic Agents for Regulated Process Automation: Challenges and Research Agenda
2606.13405.paper_summary: LLM-based agents are entering regulated industries where they automate judgment intensive quality management processes. We argue that symbolic structures already embedded in these domains, including regulations, typed process models, and compliance constraints, should be treated not merely as external monitoring mechanisms but as core architectural components that shape the agent's decision-making and behavior. We propose compliance-by-construction as a complementary paradigm to guardrail-based monitoring: a structural foundation that prevents control-flow violations, while guardrails remain essential for catching semantic errors. We identify a structured set of neuro-symbolic research challenges on foundational and capability level and show that addressing them jointly enables compliance-by-construction. We call on the neuro-symbolic community to engage with regulated process automation as a high impact research domain.
2606.13405.paper_summary_zh: LLM 基礎的代理正在進入受監管的行業，在這些行業中，它們自動化了判斷密集的質量管理過程。<lb/>我們認為，這些領域中已經嵌入的符號結構，包括法規、類型化的過程模型和合規約束，應被視為不僅僅是外部監控機制，而是塑造代理決策和行為的核心架構組件。<lb/>我們提出合規建構作為一種補充範式，以防護欄式監控：這是一個結構基礎，可以防止控制流違規，同時防護欄仍然對捕捉語義錯誤至關重要。<lb/>我們確定了一組結構化的神經符號研究挑戰，涵蓋基礎和能力層面，並展示了共同解決這些挑戰如何實現合規建構。<lb/>我們呼籲神經符號社群參與受監管的過程自動化，作為一個高影響力的研究領域。
2606.13405.author: Alexander Rombach et.al.
2606.13405.authors: Alexander Rombach, Chantale Lauer, Nijat Mehdiyev
